summary: Muddy audience tape that still captures a stellar acoustic set highlighted by Box of Rain and a soaring Dark Star.
review: The performance is praised for its historic acoustic set – especially the rare early rendition of “Box of Rain” and a powerful “Dark Star” – and the energy of the crowd.  The recording, however, is an audience (AUD) source that suffers from muffling, a high‑pitched whine and general cloudiness, making the electric portion hard to hear.  Fans love the show despite the technical flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the musical performance around 4‑5 stars (standout acoustic set, Dark Star) while consistently describing the audio as muffled, noisy or “rickety,” yielding a recording rating near 3. The weighted average (70 % show, 30 % recording) produces a ~3.7 overall.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tape with noticeable muffling and a persistent high‑pitched whine; acoustic portions clearer than electric, but overall clouded sound makes some parts hard to follow.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The acoustic half is highlighted as one of the best of 1970, featuring the full band and NRPS guests; the electric set is praised in spirit but its sound is too clouded to assess fully.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s piano on Box of Rain and his guitar tone during Dark Star are noted positively.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s vocal strength is mentioned; acoustic lead vocals highlighted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen (and Garcia) on piano add depth to the acoustic numbers; keyboards are audible but not pristine.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming described as “rocking”; Mickey Hart adds percussion on some tracks.
